title: National Reconstruction Fund Corporation backs next generation quantum computing startup Diraq with $20 million equity investment | National Reconstruction Fund Corporation
why:
- The National Reconstruction Fund Corporation is investing $20 million in Diraq, a Sydney-based quantum technology startup recognized as a global leader in utility-scale quantum computing.
- Diraq is one of only 11 companies worldwide to advance to stage B in DARPA's rigorous Quantum Benchmarking Initiative (QBI).
- Diraq’s quantum computers are built using silicon-based qubits with CMOS architecture, allowing for production at a fraction of the cost and size of competing quantum computers.
- The company's goal is to scale to millions of qubits by 2031 and deliver its first quantum computer providing genuine quantum advantage by 2029.
- The NRFC investment will fund research, product development, and commercialisation, and is expected to significantly increase Australian-based jobs in quantum technology.
- Diraq was founded in 2022 as a spinout from UNSW Sydney and currently employs over 70 staff and PhD students.
